questions.text: One factor which does not shown in ECG is:
You are evaluating an ECG of a patient, PR interval is more than 0.20sec, QRS regular. What should you suspect?
Identify the ecg: (Looking for sawtooth baseline pattern)

You are dispatched to a call for a 36 year old male construction site worker who is complaining of anxiety like symptoms at work. Upon your arrival the worker tells you that he feels palpitations, chest discomfort, vomiting, nausea, sweating and shaking. 12 lead ECG reveals no changes in relation to myocardial infarction. Based on the presentation of symptoms what is the condition of the patient
Due to which of the following conditions are cardiac abnormalities or dysrhythmias common in patients with eating disorders
options: Bleeding Of Gl tract or esophagus

questions.text: A 24-year-old male is involved in a head-on collision. If you suspect chest injury, you should:
options: Lower leg injuries, administer fluid resuscitation at 20 ml/kg
A head injury, administer an anti-seizure medication
A chest injury, obtain an EKG

questions.text: What is the most serious early complication of burns from electrical contact?
Trauma patients who presented unconscious, without palpable pulse or spontaneous respiration, found that patients with which rhythm and nondilated reactive pupils had a good chance of survival?
Which one of the following is typically associated with post-traumatic hemorrhage early shock?
options: Loss of 30% to 45% of blood volume

questions.text: A 49-year-old male was crossing the street and struck by a truck. First responders are doing CPR. The patient is pulseless, apneic and the monitor shows asystole. Your assessment reveals an open skull fracture and deformed pelvis. Based on the ITLS curriculum, which of the following is the most appropriate act?
options: Immediately intubate the patient to secure the airway
Establish intravenous access and administer a 1 liter bolus
Establish intravenous access and administer 2 mg of atropine
Resuscitative efforts should be discontinued, unless prohibited by law

questions.text: What is the dysrhythmia for a 3-year-old with a heart rate of 180 beats/min, a narrow QRS complex, and a variable R-R interval called?

A 2-year-old girl has been having respiratory distress for about an hour. She has crackles noted in her lower lobes and her lips are cyanotic. She is semi-responsive with pale and diaphoretic skin. It is difficult to obtain a BP. Other vital signs are HR 270 and RR 60. What type of shock is this patient experiencing?
What is the dysrhythmia for a 16-year-old with a heart rate of 180 beats/min, a narrow QRS complex, and a nonvariable RR interval?
A child has a heart rate between 180 - 188 beats/min with a narrow QRS and a strong pulse. Which of the following best describes his rhythm?
What is the dysrhythmia for a 16-year-old with a heart rate of 180 beats/min, a narrow QRS complex, and a non-variable RR interval? (Second instance)
A patient with a history of missed dialysis presents with tingling (paresthesia) and palpitations (irregular HR). What is the most likely condition?

A 78-year-old female presents with a sudden onset of respiratory distress. Vital signs are BP = 78/36, pulse = 110, RR = 22. Abnormal findings include crackles bilaterally, cyanosis. Which of the following medications would be most effective?
All the following are potential causes of syncope EXCEPT:

You are assessing a 69-year-old male, complaining of chest discomfort. His 12-Lead ECG reveals ST depression in leads V1-V4. There is no ST elevation in any lead. This ECG finding is most consistent with:
Symptomatic hyperkalemia should be treated with which of the following medication combinations? (Note: Hyperkalemia causes ECG changes like peaked T waves, wide QRS)
options: Sodium bicarbonate, naloxone, and calcium chloride

questions.text: A patient presents hemodynamically stable complaining of chest discomfort. A 12-Lead ECG reveals ST segment elevation in all the precordial leads as well as II, III, and aVF. Which of the following is the most likely etiology of these ECG findings?

A 52-year-old male presents complaining of chest discomfort. He is alert and oriented with a blood pressure of 110/70. You obtain the 12-Lead ECG, Shows an Inferior Wall MI (ST elevation in II, III, aVF). Of the following, the most appropriate next action would be to:
A 23-year-old female fell from a second-floor balcony. You place her on the heart monitor and it shows a wide-complex tachycardia of about 280 per minute. You should suspect:

A 16-year-old female presents complaining of difficulty breathing. Auscultation of lung fields reveals expiratory wheezing in all lung fields. Heart rate is 110. Of the following, the most likely cause:
Which of the following is most likely to present with hypertension and bradycardia?
For assessing wheezing, which method of physical examination you will use:
Standard pulse assessment includes all EXCEPT:
What is the compression-ventilation ratio for 2-rescuer child CPR?
A 5-year-old boy is wheezing and complains of chest tightness. HR is 128. What initial management should be performed?
options: Open the airway with the jaw thrust maneuver.
